headline: Brooklyn Is the City's #1 New Construction Hotspot
bullets: Brooklyn has 133 new building permits in this dataset — the most of any borough. That's more than Queens (72) and Manhattan (20) combined.
Three Brooklyn neighborhoods are tied for most new buildings: North Side–South Side, Stuyvesant Heights, and East New York — each with 10 issued permits.
Bedford, Bushwick North, and Greenpoint are also seeing serious construction. If you rent near these areas, expect more cranes soon.
sqlQuery: SELECT string_field_59, string_field_0, COUNT(*) FROM `lede-nyc-data.civicdata.dob-permits` WHERE string_field_6='NB' AND string_field_59 IS NOT NULL GROUP BY 1,2 ORDER BY 3 DESC LIMIT 20

headline: Upper West Side Leads Manhattan in Renovation Permits
bullets: A2 renovation permits dominate the Manhattan sample — 139 total, with the Upper West Side leading at 17 and Midtown-Midtown South close behind at 14.
West Village and Hudson Yards-Chelsea-Flatiron each have 11 renovation permits, pointing to high renovation activity in high-income neighborhoods.
An A2 permit means interior work — gut renovations, structural changes — that can temporarily displace tenants. Worth watching if you live in these areas.
sqlQuery: SELECT string_field_59, string_field_6, COUNT(*) FROM `lede-nyc-data.civicdata.dob-permits` WHERE string_field_6='A2' AND string_field_0='MANHATTAN' GROUP BY 1,2 ORDER BY 3 DESC LIMIT 10

headline: Queens: New Homes Going Up Fast in Flushing and South Ozone Park
bullets: Flushing leads Queens with 8 new building permits, followed by Jamaica Estates-Holliswood and South Ozone Park with 7 each.
Queens has 72 new building permits total — the second highest of any borough after Brooklyn.
College Point and Borough Park are also active, each with 6 new building permits. These are growing neighborhoods to watch.
sqlQuery: SELECT string_field_59, COUNT(*) FROM `lede-nyc-data.civicdata.dob-permits` WHERE string_field_6='NB' AND string_field_0='QUEENS' GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 2 DESC LIMIT 10

headline: 7 Buildings Being Torn Down — Mostly in Brooklyn and the Bronx
bullets: The dataset contains 7 demolition (DM) permits — 4 in Brooklyn and 3 in the Bronx. No demolitions in Manhattan or Queens in this sample.
Demolitions often precede new development. If you live in a Bronx or Brooklyn neighborhood seeing new construction, watch for DM permits nearby.
NYC DOB must approve all demolitions. You can look up any address at nyc.gov/buildings for full permit history.
sqlQuery: SELECT string_field_0, COUNT(*) FROM `lede-nyc-data.civicdata.dob-permits` WHERE string_field_6='DM' GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 2 DESC
